rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Use component type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations that reference only visible ports
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annexes; use only visible ports or variables
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Use component type annexes for type-level contracts that apply to all implementations
Use implementation annexes for implementation-specific contracts that reference subcomponents or connections
Preserve existing architecture by avoiding changes to component types or implementations beyond annex insertion
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks use the syntax `annex agree {** ... **};`
Component type annexes contain assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes contain implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in declarations
anti_patterns: Avoid using subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid mixing type-level and implementation-level references in the same annex block
Avoid using implementation-specific identifiers in component type annexes
Avoid changing the AADL architecture beyond annex insertion
